$comment: Cells of the A/B compare grid that are EXPECTED to exceed the pixel threshold,
each with the reason. This list may only SHRINK: `check` in globalTeardown.ts
fails the run when a listed cell starts passing, so a feature that lands is
forced to delete its entries. See docs/AI-VALIDATION.md.
EMPTY as of step 2.4, and that is the point. Every rendering layer at a frozen
`t` now exists in the vanilla renderer, and the whole 50-cell grid measures
EXACTLY 0.0000% — not 'under threshold', bit-identical screenshots. The
self-test independently pins the measurement's own noise floor at exactly
0.00%, so there is no headroom left to hide in: any non-zero cell is a real
difference in what the two renderers drew.
Which is why COMPARE_THRESHOLD now defaults to 0.01% instead of 0.1%. Re-adding
an entry here is a deliberate admission that a layer regressed or that a new
one has not landed yet — never a way to quiet a diff you have not explained.
